title: The Man In The Moon
content:
The Man in the Moon
                        Came tumbling down,
                    And asked his way to Norwich;



                        They told him south,
                        And he burnt his mouth
                    With eating cold pease-porridge.
meaning:
Leonard Leslie Brooke's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Man In The Moon"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
